case-for-readable-labels.label: CASE for Readable Labels
case-for-readable-labels.summary: Turn row-level values into clear categories while handling missing data before numeric thresholds.

sketch1.title: Order Thresholds from Most Specific to Broadest

single-choice-1.title: First Matching Branch
single-choice-1.prompt: How does a searched CASE choose its result?
single-choice-1.hint: CASE evaluates conditions in written order.

help.concept: The first true WHEN determines the result.
help.hint_1: Later branches are skipped after a match.
help.hint_2: Choose the behavior that makes order important.

options.b: It returns the first matching branch.

single-choice-2.title: Missing Rating Label
single-choice-2.prompt: Why should `customer_rating IS NULL` appear before rating thresholds?
single-choice-2.hint: A missing observation does not communicate the same meaning as a poor score.

help.concept: Explicit NULL handling protects the business interpretation.
help.hint_1: The report should not invent a low rating.

options.a: NULL should be treated as zero.
options.b: Missing is a separate state from low performance.

multi-choice-1.title: Good CASE Practices
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which practices improve a row-level CASE label? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-1.hint: CASE stops after the first true condition.

help.concept: Specific rules and missing-data rules should not be hidden by a broad branch.
help.hint_1: Readable labels are part of the report contract.
help.hint_2: Avoid a first branch that captures unintended rows.

options.a: Order overlapping thresholds from most specific to broadest.
options.b: Use an explicit NULL branch when missing has distinct meaning.
options.c: Choose labels that tell the reader what the category means.
options.d: Place a broad condition first so every row matches immediately.

multi-choice-2.title: Rows Labeled High Value
multi-choice-2.prompt: With `WHEN gross_sales >= 130 THEN High`, which gross values receive `High`? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-2.hint: The comparison includes the threshold itself.

help.concept: Greater-than-or-equal matches 130 and any larger value.
help.hint_1: Values below the threshold continue to later branches.

drag-reorder-1.title: Order Rating Conditions
drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the CASE checks from first to last.
drag-reorder-1.hint: Handle the distinct missing state, then test higher thresholds before lower ones.

help.concept: CASE order determines which overlapping condition wins.

fill-blank-choice-1.title: Close a CASE Expression
fill-blank-choice-1.prompt: Fill the keyword that completes the CASE before the alias.
fill-blank-choice-1.hint: A CASE expression has a required closing keyword.

help.hint_1: The alias comes after the completed CASE.

fill-blank-choice-1.template: CASE WHEN discount_pct IS NULL THEN 'Missing' ELSE 'Recorded' [blank1] AS discount_state

try_case_for_readable_labels_sketch0.title: Label Discount Review Status
try_case_for_readable_labels_sketch0.prompt: Select `order_id`, `discount_pct`, and a `discount_review` label: `Missing discount` for NULL, `Large discount` for 15 or more, otherwise `Standard discount`.
try_case_for_readable_labels_sketch0.hint: Put the NULL condition before the numeric threshold.

help.concept: CASE returns the first matching branch, so condition order communicates priority.
help.hint_2: Use ELSE for every remaining known percentage.

try_case_for_readable_labels_sketch0.solutionCode:
SELECT
    order_id,
    discount_pct,
    CASE
        WHEN discount_pct IS NULL THEN 'Missing discount'
        WHEN discount_pct >= 15 THEN 'Large discount'
        ELSE 'Standard discount'
    END AS discount_review
FROM sales_reporting;

try_case_for_readable_labels_sketch1.title: Classify Order Values
try_case_for_readable_labels_sketch1.prompt: Select `order_id`, gross sales, and `value_band`: `High value` for gross sales of 130 or more, `Mid value` for 80 or more, otherwise `Low value`.
try_case_for_readable_labels_sketch1.hint: Check the highest threshold before the lower threshold.

help.concept: Overlapping CASE conditions must be ordered so the first match is the intended label.
help.hint_2: Use ELSE for values below both thresholds.

try_case_for_readable_labels_sketch1.solutionCode:
SELECT
    order_id,
    quantity * unit_price AS gross_sales,
    CASE
        WHEN quantity * unit_price >= 130 THEN 'High value'
        WHEN quantity * unit_price >= 80 THEN 'Mid value'
        ELSE 'Low value'
    END AS value_band
FROM sales_reporting;

sketch0.title: Test Missing Values First
sketch0.bodyMarkdown:
CASE checks conditions from top to bottom and returns the first matching label. Put the missing-value branch before numeric thresholds.

```sql
SELECT
    order_id,
    customer_rating,
    CASE
        WHEN customer_rating IS NULL THEN 'Unrated'
        WHEN customer_rating >= 4.5 THEN 'Excellent'
        WHEN customer_rating >= 4.0 THEN 'Good'
        ELSE 'Needs follow-up'
    END AS rating_label
FROM sales_reporting;
```

A missing rating is not automatically a low rating, so it receives its own label.

sketch1.title: Order Thresholds from Most Specific to Broadest
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
When conditions overlap, test the highest or most specific threshold first.

```sql
SELECT
    order_id,
    quantity * unit_price AS gross_sales,
    CASE
        WHEN quantity * unit_price >= 150 THEN 'Large'
        WHEN quantity * unit_price >= 90 THEN 'Standard'
        ELSE 'Small'
    END AS order_size
FROM sales_reporting;
```

If the `>= 90` branch came first, values above 150 would never reach the `Large` branch.
